text.title: pytest Async Testing — Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed when python-pytest-async is mis-applied, each with symptom, root cause, and concrete fix.

antipattern.reason: Root cause: Coroutine object is the fixture value; tests get a coroutine instead of a client. Fix: Use @pytest_asyncio.fixture.

antipattern.description: mocker.patch('x', return_value=...) on an async callable.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: RuntimeWarning: coroutine was never awaited; test passes silently. Fix: Use mocker.AsyncMock or new=AsyncMock(return_value=...).
Root cause: Blocks the loop; test hangs. Fix: Wrap in sync_to_async or move to a sync test.

antipattern.description: No asyncio_mode set; tests inherit default.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Strict mode silently skips unmarked async tests. Fix: Set asyncio_mode='auto' or strict explicitly.
